prerequisites: A destination for the data: either a local DuckDB file path or a MotherDuck-hosted database (use MotherDuck when running in Airbyte Cloud or Kubernetes, since local file-based databases are not supported there).
A MotherDuck account if you target a MotherDuck database, so you can create an access token (the motherduck_api_key).
The destination_path value: a local file path (it is always rooted at /local, mapped onto the host's local mount) or a MotherDuck database URI using the md: prefix.
Optionally, the name of the schema to write tables into (the schema config field).

authMethods.name: MotherDuck access token
authMethods.summary: For MotherDuck-hosted destinations, provide your MotherDuck Service Credential as the motherduck_api_key config field. Create the token in the MotherDuck UI by opening the organization Settings and clicking Create token (tokens can be Read/Write or Read Scaling). Airbyte recommends supplying it via this setting rather than embedding it in the md: connection string, to avoid leaking the token to logs.

authMethods.name: No credentials (local file)
authMethods.summary: When writing to a local DuckDB file, no authentication is required. Leave motherduck_api_key empty and set destination_path to the local file path; data is written under the host's local mount.

setupSteps.title: Choose local file or MotherDuck
setupSteps.body: Decide whether the destination is a local DuckDB file or a MotherDuck-hosted database. Local file-based databases do not work in Airbyte Cloud or Kubernetes, so use MotherDuck in those environments.

setupSteps.title: Set destination_path
setupSteps.body: For a local file, set destination_path to a path such as /local/destination.duckdb; it is always rooted at /local and mapped onto the host local mount (default /tmp/airbyte_local via the LOCAL_ROOT env variable). For MotherDuck, set destination_path to your database URI using the md: prefix.

setupSteps.title: Create a MotherDuck access token (MotherDuck only)
setupSteps.body: In the MotherDuck UI, click your organization name then Settings, click Create token, name it, choose the token type (Read/Write by default, or Read Scaling) and expiry, then copy the token.

setupSteps.title: Provide the MotherDuck API key (MotherDuck only)
setupSteps.body: Paste the MotherDuck access token into the motherduck_api_key config field rather than embedding it in the md: connection string.

setupSteps.title: Set the schema (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set the schema config field to control which schema tables are written into.
